verdict_reasoning: Fibronectin is secreted as a soluble plasma dimer but is also assembled into a fibrillar matrix on the cell surface via integrin (α5β1)-mediated stepwise polymerization. The assembled fibronectin matrix is wash-resistant, covalently/non-covalently cross-linked into ECM fibrils, and stably associated with the cell surface — not in equilibrium with the soluble pool. Clinical programs (e.g., ED-B fibronectin-targeted radioligands, ADCs, immunocytokines like L19-based agents) target the surface/matrix-deposited form on tumor stroma. This fits stable_surface_attachment via integrin-templated matrix assembly, though matrix deposition is borderline with secreted_only.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Boundary between cell-surface fibrillar assembly (stable_surface_attachment) vs stromal ECM deposition (secreted_only); ED-B isoform clinical targeting supports surface accessibility.
